title: init_db() never called — running service 500s on first request (no 'books' table)
evidence: app.py:38 defines init_db() but it is called nowhere; __main__ (app.py:272-275) only runs app.run(). On a fresh books.db the table is never created, so GET/POST /books raise sqlite3.OperationalError: no such table: books. Tests pass only because test_app.py:31 creates the schema itself and monkey-patches get_db.
suggestion: Call init_db() inside an app context at startup (e.g. with app.app_context(): init_db()) before app.run().

title: Unused 'import json' in app.py
evidence: app.py:3 imports json but it is never referenced (responses use flask.jsonify); grep 'json\.' app.py returns nothing.

title: reset_db()/_testing_db test hooks are dead code
evidence: app.py:15,20-21,56-73 provide a _testing_db/reset_db path, but test_app.py:44-52 bypasses it by monkey-patching app_module.get_db directly, so _testing_db is always None and reset_db() is never invoked.
suggestion: Either wire tests to use reset_db()/_testing_db, or delete the unused hooks.
